differences.title: 1. EXECUTION MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Compiles JSX to highly optimized vanilla JS. No VDOM.
differences.rightDesc: Pauses and resumes execution without re-rendering. Code splitting.

differences.title: 2. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Fine-grained reactivity via signals and stores. Minimal overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Leverages context and dedicated state primitives for efficient sharing.

differences.title: 3. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: Outputs minimal, efficient vanilla JS, no framework overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Loads only necessary JS to resume interactive. Instant TTI.

differences.title: 4. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Updates the DOM directly without a virtual DOM diffing step.
differences.rightDesc: Smart hydration that picks up where the server left off.

differences.title: 5. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Reactions update only what's needed. Highly performant.
differences.rightDesc: Resumability enables controlled, efficient updates without re-execution.

differences.title: 6. MEMORY USAGE
differences.leftDesc: Minimal memory overhead due to compiler and reactivity model.
differences.rightDesc: Avoids storing large amounts of application state on the client.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Strong community, gaining traction for performance-focused apps.
differences.rightDesc: Newer, focused on novel resumability and performance paradigms.
